question: Over $400M committed to the Monad public sale?
event_title: Monad public sale total commitments above ___ ?

evidences.title: Monad Token Public Sale Launched on Coinbase, Market ...
evidences.content: Derived from PANews, the public token sale of Monad, a new high-performance Layer 1 blockchain, has officially begun on Coinbase's new token offering platform on November 17. The sale will last until November 22, with tokens expected to be distributed on November 24. As the first project on Coinbase's new platform, Monad is seen as a key experiment in transparent and compliant token issuance. The sale price is set at $0.025 per MON token, with a total of 7.5 billion tokens offered, raising up to $187.5 million. The sale is open to over 80 countries, including Hong Kong, and requires USDC as the only accepted payment method. Coinbase has implemented a fair allocation mechanism prioritizing small investors and limiting large allocations. However, the initial response has been relatively [...] However, the initial response has been relatively slow compared to previous token sales, with only 45% of tokens subscribed within the first six hours. Market prediction platform Polymarket suggests the final raise is likely to fall between $400 million and $1 billion. Meanwhile, Monad's testnet has already launched in 25 countries with 174 validators, and over 300 projects have joined its ec...

evidences.title: Monad Token Debuts on Coinbase at $3.9 Billion Fully Diluted ...
evidences.content: Monad’s native token MON successfully launched on Coinbase with a $3.9 billion fully diluted valuation (FDV), marking a significant milestone in the cryptocurrency market. The Layer-1 blockchain project completed its initial token offering on Coinbase’s new token sale platform before transitioning to public trading. The token sale exceeded expectations by raising $269 million from more than 85,800 participants across 70+ countries. This impressive participation level demonstrates strong investor confidence in Monad’s vision and technology, despite initial concerns about the pace of the sale. [...] > .@Monad went live less than 24 hours ago > > It already cleared: > – 3.7M daily txns > – 153K active addresses > – 18K contract deployments > > That’s higher day-one activity than most chains in their first year > > 🧵 👇 pic.twitter.com/ggUwfOyjx7 > > — Nansen 🧭 (@nansen\_ai) November 25, 2025 ## Record-Breaking Token Sale Monad’s token sale offered 7.5 billion MON tokens at $0.025 each, representing 7.5% of the total 100 billion supply. Although the sale started somewhat slowly, it eventually attracted widespread interest and became oversubscribed by 1.43x, with commitments exceeding...

evidences.title: Monad Launches Public Sale on Coinbase's New Token Sale Platform
evidences.content: This week, the Monad public token sale on Coinbase has become one of the tests so far of whether large, high fully diluted valuation (FDV) L1/L2 launches still command investor interest in a more regulated and more skeptical market. As of writing, the sale has raised a little almost $150 million, roughly 80% of its $187.5 million target at $0.025 per MON, with about two and a half days still to run and 7.5% of the 100 billion token supply on offer. We expect the sale to end up oversubscribed, but the order of magnitude is noticeably different from the recent L2 MegaETH public sale that targeted $50 million and drew around $1.4 billion of bids, roughly 28 times the goal. Token launches are clearly back in 2025, yet we see shifting investor behavior. The market is still willing to fund [...] Monad’s offering follows the familiar high FDV and low float pattern, which we view as the core mid-term risk. The initial 100 billion supply allocates 7.5% to the public sale, 3.3% to an airdrop, 38.5% to ecosystem development, 27% to the team, 19.7% to investors, and 4% to the treasury, leaving only about 10.8% in public circulation at mainnet launch, while another 38.5% sits unlocked under...

evidences.title: Monad Breaks Records on Coinbase Token Sale Amid ...
evidences.content: Monad's token sale marked the first on Coinbase's new platform, highlighting significant progress in blockchain decentralization and market engagement. The strategic decision to skip a pre-deposit campaign and strong participant turnout reflect a calculated approach to ecosystem growth. Monad's native token, MON, slipped due to profit-taking after reaching $0.025, but a $100 million private credit vault is expected to boost TVL. The token was trading near $0.021 after falling about 7% over the past 24 hours. [...] The MON token saw a price decline due to profit-taking, with renewed interest in the $0.025 level triggering sell activity. Despite this, the launch of a $100 million private-credit vault on Accountable's Yield App, fully verifiable on-chain, is expected to support TVL growth. The TVL on Monad has already surpassed $250 million, signaling growing institutional interest. [...] The Monad Foundation has hired senior executives from Optimism, FalconX, and BVNK to drive institutional adoption and market expansion. These hires include Urvit Goel, Joanita Titan, and Sagar Sarbhai, who bring traditional finance and institutional crypto infrastructure experience. Monad's token...

evidences.title: Monad to raise $188m in Coinbase's first public token sale
evidences.content: Last month, rival blockchain project MegaETH sold $50 million worth of its native token at a $1 billion valuation through Sonar, a token sale venue tied to early-stage investing platform Echo. The sale’s popularity vastly exceeded expectations, attracting over $300 million worth of pledged capital. Monad’s token sale ups the ante by valuing the project at $2.5 billion. Some 7.5% of the MON token’s 100 billion starting supply is up for grabs at $0.025 per token. The MON token is used to pay gas fees on Monad. Once the blockchain launches, MON holders can stake their tokens to secure the network. [...] On Monday, the upcoming blockchain project announced it will sell $188 million worth of tokens to retail investors on Coinbase through a newly announced token sale platform. According to Monad, the sale will help decentralise the project by distributing its MON token more broadly among its community. For retail investors, it’s another opportunity to get exposure to a high-risk bet in an unlaunched crypto project. Monad has previously raised a total of $248 million over multiple funding rounds, according to Tracxn, a market data platform. This includes a $19 million seed round in ear...

At the start of Monad Public Mainnet, 50.6B tokens (50.6%) will be locked. Locked tokens cannot be staked. The intent of this is twofold: to ensure that staking rewards initially contribute to the public circulation of MON and to avoid an initial concentration of rewards among those individuals (team members and investors) involved directly with the project. evidences.content: The sale initially raised $43 million in just 30 minutes, but by the end of the second day had raised just $120 million or 68% of its target. evidences.title: Monad Raises $128.7M in Coinbase Token Presale
evidences.content: A temporary halt occurred in March 2025 when a supermajority was reached at an epoch boundary. The issue was resolved, and stability returned. In September 2025, Monad open-sourced its execution client under the GPL-3.0 license. Previously, in September 2024, the Discord server link was temporarily compromised but later secured. ## Why the Raise Matters The presale demonstrates robust market interest in high-performance Layer 1 solutions. It reflects confidence in EVM compatibility, parallel execution, and scalable consensus mechanisms. Coinbase’s token sales platform allows distributed ownership of MON tokens. A broad allocation aims to prevent concentration among large purchasers. If oversubscription occurs, the “filling up from the bottom” mechanism ensures equitable distribution. [...] The sale is the first direct token offering hosted on Coinbase. It is open to participants in over 80 countries, including the U.S. Early-stage activity was steady but slower than expected. Analysts attribute this to investors waiting for final moments to commit larger funds. Currently, the sale sees an additional $693,500 added each hour. The public sale will close on 23 November at 10:00, ju...

evidences.title: Monad's MON Token Makes Weak Trading Debut as ... - CoinDesk
evidences.content: Trading activity was subdued. In the first 100 minutes, MON saw only $50 million in trading volume, less than is typical for a layer-1 token debut and a sign that demand may be softer than expected. The cool start follows an underwhelming public token sale on Coinbase’s Token Platform. Of the circulating supply, 7.5% was allocated to the sale at $0.025 per token, higher than where MON is currently trading. Many recent token launches have been snapped up almost instantly, most notably Plasma, which sold out within the first block. In contrast, MON’s sale took significantly longer to clear. That might be a signal of a lack of demand that appears to be a consistent theme with the trading debut. [...] # Monad’s MON Token Stumbles Out of the Gate in Trading Debut After Slow Token Sale ## Soft demand, low volume and concerns over token distribution weighed on early market sentiment. Google Monad stumbles out the gate (Dallas Reedy/Unsplash)") #### What to know: The MON token for the newly introduced Monad blockchain made its trading debut on Monday, but early market activity suggests a lukewarm reception for one of the year’s most anticipated layer-1 blockchains. evidences.title: Maxim Krasnov's Post - LinkedIn
evidences.content: This is a masterclass in how NOT to launch a Tier-1 token. Monad raised a record-breaking $400M. Now it is trading below its presale price. What happened to the "Solana Killer"? Let’s look at the numbers and the execution. 1. The Artificial Pump The sale valuation was $2.5B (0.025 per token). To ensure the Coinbase listing didn't look like a flop, Market Maker aggressively pushed the FDV up to $4.8B. 2. The Reality Check Once the MM stopped propping up the price, the uncomfortable truth came out: The ecosystem is empty. — Failed Incentives: The Monad Momentum program (designed to distribute $MON to protocols to attract users) has gone silent. No user acquisition is happening yet. — Liquidity Issues: On-chain pools are largely paired with USDC, not the native $MON token. This kills the [...] 119 50 Comments Like Comment Richard Keskküla 1w Report this comment A four hundred million dollar raise and a tier one listing should have been a launchpad, not an anchor. Yet here we are, trading below presale because the market finally priced in what the hype ignored. The MM pump pushed FDV to almost five billion, but once support faded, the real picture surfaced. No active incentive engin...

evidences.title: Coinbase Acquires Echo for $400M, Betting on Community and ...
evidences.content: Coinbase is democratising access to early-stage crypto investments whilst building in the safeguards that were missing during the Wild West days. The bigger question is whether this works. Are we witnessing the maturation of an idea that crashed spectacularly, or just history repeating itself with better PR? Coinbase is betting that with proper oversight, transparent rules, and strategic positioning in crypto-friendly territory, they can capture the innovation of the old ICO days without the chaos. The Monad Foundation sale runs 17th-22nd November. Whether it succeeds could determine how the next generation of blockchain projects raises money - and where they choose to do it. Evolution or repetition? We're about to find out. [...] On Tuesday morning, I was scrolling through crypto news and spotted something that hasn't happened since 2017. Coinbase just announced they're bringing back public token sales - but this time with a twist that's got the entire crypto world watching. Remember the ICO craze of 2017? Everyone was launching cryptocurrencies. Billions poured in before projects crashed, disappeared, or turned out to be outright scams. Regulators cracked down hard, and public...

evidences.title: Monad's Mission: Why Monad?
evidences.content: MON is the Monad network’s native token, used to pay gas fees, secure the chain via staking, and align validators, developers, and users around the growth of the protocol. At the launch of Monad Public Mainnet, the total initial supply of MON will be 100 billion tokens. Up to 7.5B $MON tokens (7.5% of initial token supply) will be made available to the public for purchase in the public sale at a price of $0.025 per token. The Coinbase token sales platform accommodates a minimum bid size of $100 and a maximum bid size of $100,000 (subject to higher maximum thresholds for Coinbase One subscribers pursuant to the Coinbase token sale platform terms). [...] Coinbase token sales has provided a platform for the Monad Foundation to distribute MON through a public sale via its subsidiary, MF Services (BVI), Ltd., enabling the purchase of MON tokens right before the launch of Monad Public Mainnet on November 24th, 2025. It will open on Monday, November 17th, 2025 at 9:00 AM ET and run until Saturday, November 22nd, 2025 at 9:00 PM ET. Those interested in learning more or participating in the public sale can do so here. Monad’s mission is to power a new world economy, suitable for a world...

evidences.title: Coinbase's Monad public token sale starts hot and then fizzles | The Block
evidences.content: Although arguably off to a lackluster start, the Monad token could still prove to be a seminal moment for not only Coinbase but also crypto as a whole. After last decade's initial coin offering, or ICO, boom attracted some fraudsters seeking to take advantage of investors, the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission began cracking down in 2018. The SEC classified many tokens as unregistered securities, which effectively ended the ICO era and pushed most new token launches offshore or into private sales limited to accredited investors.
